Transcrição Completa: When Is The Time To Fire A Client

[00:00] All right. So, it was November of 2017 and here I am. I had too many staff. I had too many of the wrong clients and I knew it was time that I was going to have to fire some people both in my staff and in my client roster in order to free myself up.

[00:20] I'd burdened my time. I'd woken up every day for about three months straight. Had none none of my personal freedom. no ability to even meditate in the morning from how fast I had to leave the house just to get to the office to beat everybody else there to prepare for the day to be in business and to get results and to maximize my payroll costs that I had with the amount of staff that I had.

[00:45] I had to still focus on sales. I had to still focus on bringing in new deals. I had to focus on paying everybody else's bills through payroll and all the expenses and liabilities that came with that in addition to managing the clients, the relationships, and the results that we were getting for all these different people while being wildly underpaid myself.

[01:10] It was too much work and not enough reward for myself and my own internal ratio. And I just overall wasn't that happy. So, I needed to fire some clients. So, I come into the office one day just almost depressed, right? you know, this weight on my shoulders, high levels of stress, and just overall definitely not in a state of happiness.

[01:35] So, I made the decision walking into the office that day that it was time to make some uncomfortable decisions. I was going to need to refund $109,000, $19,000 worth of clients. That was a substantial amount of money.

[01:50] Although we were doing about $220,000 a month, firing $109,000 worth of those clients would have taken me just above the amount that I was expensing in both payroll costs, rent, and just overall taxes that I had to factor into the money that we were making.

[02:10] So, long story short, firing that amount of clients also was going to require me to fire some staff. So, I had to go and let almost all my sales team go besides the most qualified people that I could put into different positions in the company.

[02:25] I pivoted the internal operations of my business at the time to focus on much more specialized positions that we were going to start servicing higher ticket clients with. And I had to make these changes in such a short duration of one day from the true level of almost desperation and true depression that I was at in my own life and business at that point.

[02:50] So, I said, "You know what? It's worth it and I'm going to be in a better position because I'm taking actions that are moving me forward and it's going to be a necessity for my success and my agency's success in the both short and long haul of this."

[03:05] So, I made the decision, come in, log into Stripe, refund, refund, refund, refund, refund. This was by my choice, not my client's choice. I then wrote a nice email explaining to the clients that we were letting them go.

[03:20] I put in each person's email whatever we had created for them including social content funnels just overall documentation of whatever we had done to that point. I then made my staff educated that I was going to be letting key people go and one person after the other coming into my office explaining to them that I was going to have to let them go.

[03:40] Sometimes even paying off a severance package to different people upwards of $30,000 in total between different staff members for just money I had to pay out to make sure these people would be financially well off and not cut off completely at the same time.

[03:55] So, long story short, this ends up costing me over $139,000 just to refund clients and fire staff with a severance package and let them go. I end up with a key staff of about eight people at that point.

[04:10] And we ended up going from about 80,000ish a month to upwards of $200,000 a month again in just under 45 days from simply firing the wrong clients and making room for the right clients.

[04:25] I want to be very direct with you here. It was probably one of the most uncomfortable things that I've ever had to do. And it was a more rock bottom moment for me to almost be forced to make that kind of decision rather than where I talk to you at now where I'm much more in control of my business in control of the decisions I'm making and more importantly overall in control of my happiness levels for what I choose to put my time, energy, and attention towards in my business in my agency.

[04:55] It's led me today to do just over in given the month for how much money we make. People hundreds of thousands, sometimes 75,000 to 100,000 at the lowest depending on where we're at with our clients at the time. We currently work maybe one to five people a month tops.

[05:15] A majority of the deals we do now are consulting deals versus hands-on deals. They require such a small amount of time and effort and they're much more result driven. My staff are only key players. Those eight people have been whittled down to about five through time and they're all people who I absolutely enjoy working with and have great relationships with.

[05:35] Some are even equity owners in different companies that I have on a percentage basis. So my point being is I went from low levels of trust for my staff, low levels of happiness, just overall low levels of fulfillment with what I was doing in a day to refunding and firing different clients and staff.

[05:55] Doing it the ethical way, the responsible way, making sure that I didn't pocket the money, making sure I made people made sure people were financially well off. And I understand not everybody who might be in that same position can do that to where you can still be financially afloat, refund that amount of money, and also fire clients, I mean, fire staff at the same time and pay out severance packages.

[06:20] But to be clear, no matter your situation, if you know that you have the wrong clients and if you know that you're in a position of unhealthy business operations, it's time to trim the fat and it's time to make the necessary decisions that you know you need to make in order to progress in your business.

[06:40] For me, the void existed for about 30 to 45 days. But in that same duration of time, I took positive progressive steps forward, including pitching the right clients, including telling my key staff members what the plan was for the future and then materializing on that plan and making it real.

[07:00] So my point is when you take the right actions after removing and cutting out the wrong actions and the wrong people, you'll have progress that's made. It's about progress, not perfection.

[07:10] Don't operate in a state of unhealthy and just overall unhappiness in your business. that'll hurt you in the long run and in the short term. And that's not what you're here for. You're here to do an empowering service of solving problems for business owners, helping them make more leads and drive more revenues, empower your own goals, you know, materialize things in your life.

[07:35] Help people inspire the economy and those around you to be better and, you know, more stimulated. It's an incredible thing that you do as a business owner when you're successful and happy. Don't ever sacrifice that. Make the necessary cuts when it's time to. I'll see you in the next video.

📊 RESUMO EXECUTIVO

NÚMEROS PRINCIPAIS:

Novembro de 2017: Momento da decisão crítica

$109,000: Valor total em reembolsos de clientes

$220,000/mês: Receita mensal antes dos cortes

$80,000/mês: Receita após cortes iniciais

$200,000/mês: Receita recuperada em 45 dias

$30,000: Total pago em pacotes de indenização

$139,000: Custo total (reembolsos + indenizações)

8 pessoas: Equipe reduzida inicialmente

5 pessoas: Equipe final otimizada

30-45 dias: Período de recuperação

$75,000-$100,000+/mês: Receita atual mínima

1-5 clientes/mês: Volume atual de trabalho

3 meses: Duração do período de burnout antes da decisão

ESTRATÉGIAS E SISTEMAS:

Processo de Reestruturação:

Identificação de clientes errados e excesso de staff

Decisão de corte radical em um único dia

Reembolso total via Stripe

Entrega de todos os ativos criados (conteúdo social, funis, documentação)

Demissão ética com pacotes de indenização

Transição para posições especializadas

Foco em clientes high-ticket

Modelo de Negócio Transformado:

De serviços hands-on para consultoria

De muitos clientes low-ticket para poucos clientes high-ticket

De equipe grande para equipe enxuta e qualificada

De operações complexas para especializadas

Estrutura de Equipe:

Eliminação quase total do time de vendas

Retenção apenas dos mais qualificados

Reposicionamento em funções especializadas

Programa de equity para key players

ESTRUTURA:

Fase de Crise (Novembro 2017):

Staff excessivo

Clientes errados em excesso

Zero liberdade pessoal

Sem tempo para meditação matinal

Múltiplas responsabilidades simultâneas: vendas, gestão, resultados, folha de pagamento

Subpagamento pessoal

Alto estresse e depressão

Fase de Transição (1 dia + 30-45 dias):

Dia 1: Decisão e execução dos cortes

Reembolsos massivos

Comunicação ética com clientes

Demissões com indenizações

Redução de equipe

Período de "vazio" de 30-45 dias

Fase de Crescimento (Pós 45 dias):

Recuperação para $200k/mês

Modelo de consultoria predominante

Equipe reduzida a 5 pessoas

Alto nível de controle e felicidade

Relacionamentos de qualidade com staff

Estrutura de equity partnerships

DADOS FINANCEIROS:

Antes da Reestruturação:

Receita: $220,000/mês

Despesas: Folha de pagamento elevada + aluguel + impostos

Lucro pessoal: Abaixo do esperado ("wildly underpaid")

ROI de tempo: Negativo (trabalho excessivo, recompensa insuficiente)

Custo da Reestruturação:

Reembolsos de clientes: $109,000

Indenizações: $30,000

Total investido na mudança: $139,000

Após Reestruturação (45 dias):

Queda inicial: ~$80,000/mês

Recuperação: $200,000/mês

Crescimento: +150% em 45 dias

Estado Atual:

Receita mínima: $75,000-$100,000/mês

Receita típica: Centenas de milhares/mês

Volume: 1-5 clientes/mês

Modelo: Majoritariamente consultoria (alto ticket, baixo esforço)

Equipe: 5 pessoas chave com equity

ROI de tempo: Altamente positivo

Nível de felicidade: Alto controle e satisfação

Métricas de Transformação:

Redução de equipe: ~60% (de 8+ para 5)

Aumento de eficiência: Menos clientes, mais receita

Tempo de recuperação: 45 dias

Investimento na mudança: $139,000

Retorno: Sustentabilidade a longo prazo + qualidade de vida

DURAÇÃO: ~7:50 minutos FORMATO: Vídeo educacional / Case study pessoal / Lição de negócios
